title: Iranian parliament speaker accuses US of planning ground attack
subhead: Speaking two days after the USS Tripoli, an amphibious assault ship carrying US marines, arrived in the Middle East, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said the US was 'secretly planning a ground attack.'

title: Escaping Putin's Russia: A father and daughter persecuted over a drawing, now in exile in France
Emmanuel Grégoire inaugurated mayor of Paris
Israeli police prevent Jerusalem's Latin Patriarch from entering Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday
subhead: French President Emmanuel Macron condemned this action, saying that worship 'for all religions' must be guaranteed in Jerusalem. Israeli police said all holy sites in Jerusalem have been closed since the start of the Middle East war.

title: Two more suspects arrested in foiled Paris bomb attack outside Bank of America
subhead: Police had detained a first suspect, possibly a minor, in the early hours of Saturday just after he placed a device outside the bank building near the Champs-Elysées. He was accompanied by a second person who fled when police arrived.

title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
subhead: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.

title: Anne Hidalgo's unwavering tenacity after 12 years as Paris mayor
subhead: After being elected to the Council of Paris in 2001, Hidalgo was immediately appointed as then-mayor Bertrand Delanoë's first deputy, before becoming mayor herself in 2014. Over her two terms in office, her determination, despite strong political opposition and legal obstacles, set her apart.

In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
subhead: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

title: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
subhead: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

title: The US and Israel are fighting different wars against the same foe
subhead: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu share many objectives. However, their respective public opinions and electoral interests may ultimately highlight significant differences between the two leaders.

title: Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
